events.payload:
**Arc**: After researching Claude Cowork's storage layout and confirming the JSONL schema is identical to Claude Code, the AI identified two structural blockers (discovery path and cwd-anchoring) and outlined a concrete fix involving changes to the synthesizer agent prompt.

**Before**: AI proposed two specific next steps before implementing: (1) write a spec or open a feature issue in `.scratch/`, and (2) run a minimal validation script to dump all Cowork sessions on the machine and confirm `userSelectedFolders` is reliable across multiple sessions before committing to the approach.

**After ÔÇö concrete artifacts**: AI read `packages/cli/src/index.ts` (T38), `packages/core/src/storage.ts` (T40), then made 11 edits to `work-memory-claude-plugin/agents/work-log-synthesizer.md` (T49-T70), plus edits to `README.md` (T75, T77) and `CHANGELOG.md` (T79). No spec file was created, no feature issue was opened, and no Cowork session dump script was written.

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) AI named two specific concrete options ÔÇö a spec/feature-issue and a validation dump script ÔÇö specific enough to be identifiable artifacts; (b) user did not address either option and instead asked whether it could simply be added directly; (c) subsequent work (T34-T85) consisted entirely of code reading and synthesizer prompt edits, with no spec, no issue, and no validation script produced.

**Arc**: The synthesizer prompt changes were complete and had been verified via a headless `claude -p` run that produced a `synthesized 1 entries` result with `agent_backend: cowork`. The AI presented a summary of what was done and offered next steps.

**Before**: AI proposed three specific named options: (1) run `/work-memory:work-report` to see the entry projected as a daily report, (2) decide whether to keep or delete the test project `mock-user/mock-org/agent-production`, and (3) commit the changes (noting it would open a branch first).

**After ÔÇö concrete artifacts**: AI ran `npm run build` in the `electron-example/` directory (T134-T135), launched `./node_modules/.bin/electron .` as a background task (T137), read the Electron log output (T139-T140, T145-T146), read `electron-example/src/main/sessions.ts` (T149-T150). None of the three proposed options (work-report, project cleanup, commit) was executed during this window.

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) AI offered ÔëÑ2 named concrete options ÔÇö running `/work-memory:work-report`, deleting or keeping `mock-user/mock-org/agent-production`, and committing on a new branch ÔÇö each identifiable and actionable; (b) user did not address any of them and instead asked to start the Electron client for manual GUI testing; (c) the next 10+ turns (T87-T141) were entirely about building, launching, and observing the Electron application, with no report generated, no project deleted, and no commit made.
